body:
Currently `--override.osaka` doesn't work because it only overrides with the provided time, but does not provide a blob schedule. Since the blob schedule isn't provided on any network yet, it will fail to start. Even after we add the Osaka blob schedule to the default networks, it usefulness is very limited since 1) it alone cannot change the max or target for the fork and 2) there are several BPOs scheduled alongside Osaka with their own max and targets - none of which can be overridden yet.

For those reasons, I propose removing the `OverrideOsaka` flag and pursue a complete / future-proof solution such as #32556.
title: all: remove osaka and bpo override flags
core: don't overwrite stored chain config with default based on genesis hash

body:
This implements the conversion of existing blob transactions to the new proof 
version. Conversion is triggered at the Osaka fork boundary. The conversion is 
designed to be idempotent, and may be triggered multiple times in case of a reorg 
around the fork boundary. 

This change is the last missing piece that completes our strategy for the blobpool 
conversion. After the Osaka fork,

- new transactions will be converted on-the-fly upon entry to the pool
- reorged transactions will be converted while being reinjected
- (this change) existing transactions will be converted in the background

This is a resubmission of #32665 with some edits.
title: core/txpool/blobpool: fork boundary conversion 3

body:
As reported by @pk910 when doing an eth_call post Osaka and not providing any gas limit, geth will assume the rpc cap (50m). However that ends up being higher than the new tx gas limit (EIP-7825) and causes an error.

- We should disable the tx gas limit check when doing eth_call and debug_traceCall (how we disable the nonce check etc.).
- As for eth_estimateGas I'd argue it's a different case. We should change it so that the high bound it starts with is the tx gas limit.
- As for eth_simulate the gas limit check should be disabled for normal mode, but enabled for the strict validation mode.
title: eth_call default gas limit fails post-Osaka

This PR fixes it and adds the Prague and Osaka specs.
title: params: update config description links to new format

body:
This pull request introduces a queue for legacy sidecar conversion to handle 
transactions that persist after the Osaka fork. Simply dropping these transactions 
would significantly harm the user experience.

To balance usability with system complexity, we have introduced a conversion 
time window of two hours post Osaka fork. During this period, the system will 
accept legacy blob transactions and convert them in a background process. 
After the window, all legacy transactions will be rejected. Notably, all the blob
transactions will be validated statically before the conversion, and also all 
conversion are performed in a single thread, minimize the risk of being DoS.

We believe this two hour window provides sufficient time to process in-flight 
legacy transactions and allows submitters to migrate to the new format.
title: core/txpool/blobpool: introduce sidecar conversion for legacy blob transactions

body:
This pull request adds the conversion for the legacy sidecar if these transactions are

title: core/txpool/blobpool: add legacy sidecar conversion before re-injection

body:
As a consequence of moving blob sidecar version migration code around, we ended up building blocks
with a mix of v0 and v1  blob transactions (different proof encoding in the sidecar).

This PR makes sure we are not building illegal blocks after Osaka. Blob migration is left for another PR.

Related issues and PRs:
- https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/31791
- https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/32347
- https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/31966
- https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/issues/32235
title: core/txpool/blobpool: Do not use blobs with wrong sidecar version while building block after Osaka

body:
#### System information

Geth version: 1.16.3

#### Expected behaviour

Nothing should have changed before Osaka being activated. Max blobs per tx should remain at 7.

#### Actual behaviour

New limitation of 6 blobs is applied due to https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/32246's global param being used in the txpool validation.

#### Backtrace

Since the upgrade to 1.16.3 on Ethereum Sepolia, our L2 blob transactions get rejected with this error:

```
err=\"transaction blob limit exceeded: blob count 7, limit 6\""
```
title: Osaka's Max blobs per transactions new value is applied to before proper hardfork

body:
This pull request implements #32235 , constructing blob sidecar in new format (cell proof)
if the Osaka has been activated.

Apart from that, it introduces a pre-conversion step in the blob pool before adding the txs.
This mechanism is essential for handling the remote **legacy** blob txs from the network.

One thing is still missing and probably is worthy being highlighted here: the blobpool may
contain several legacy blob txs before the Osaka and these txs should be converted once
Osaka is activated. While the `GetBlob` API in blobpool is capable for generating cell proofs 
at the runtime, converting legacy txs at one time is much cheaper overall.
title: core, internal, miner, signer: convert legacy sidecar in Osaka fork

body:
Reposting from Gary https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/32347#discussion_r2253280001

> During the Osaka activation, we should somehow convert the legacy sidecars to new format. It should be done in a following PR.
title: Convert existing legacy sidecars to new proof format

body:
BlobSidecarV1 is introduced in Osaka fork, with a different proof mechanism (cell proof).

In RPC, the default proof scheme should be changed to V1 if user tries to submit the blob
transactions there, fork awareness is required for dispatching the behavior.

Besides, the proof conversion is required if users calculate the proof by themselves in
the legacy manner.
title: RPC fork awareness

body:
# Title  
Request to Lower Gas Fees to Stay Competitive  

# Rationale  
Ethereum gas fees are currently much higher than those on competing blockchains. This discourages adoption and impacts usability, especially for:  
- Developers building small-scale or experimental projects.  
- Applications requiring frequent or microtransactions (DeFi, NFTs, gaming, etc.).  
- End-users who are priced out due to high costs.  

Lowering gas fees would make Ethereum more accessible, inclusive, and competitive, ultimately strengthening the ecosystem and retaining developers and users who might otherwise migrate to cheaper alternatives.  

# Implementation  
Possible directions could include:  
- Protocol-level optimizations to improve throughput and efficiency.  
- Mechanisms that better balance network security with affordability.  
- Clearer roadmap or communication about ongoing efforts to reduce costs.  

I am not proposing a specific implementation, but I am raising this request to emphasize the importance of addressing gas fee competitiveness. I would be willing to contribute to testing, feedback, or community discussions around potential solutions.
title: Request to Lower Gas Fees to Stay Competitive

body:
This PR makes 2 changes to how [EIP-7825](https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/31824) behaves. 

When `eth_estimateGas` or `eth_createAccessList` is called without any gas limit in the payload, geth will choose the block's gas limit or the `RPCGasCap`, which can be larger than the `maxTxGas`. 

When this happens for `estimateGas`, the gas estimation just errors out and ends, when it should continue doing binary search to find the lowest possible gas limit. 

This PR will: 
- Add a check to see if `hi` is larger than `maxTxGas` and cap it to `maxTxGas` if it's larger. And add a special case handling for gas estimation execute when it errs with `ErrGasLimitTooHigh`

- Decrease the `RPCGasCap` to 30M (== maxTxGas) to make `createAccessList` choose 30M as the highest gas cap for the tx. This can also be fixed by adding a check like this to use `maxTxGas` when the specified `RPCGasCap` is larger than `maxTxGas`. I'd be glad to fix it either way!
```
gasCap :=b.RPCGasCap()  
if gasCap > params.MaxTxGas { 
    gasCap = params.MaxTxGas
}
```

I've created this PR for fusaka-devnet-3 first, and will open another pr to master (or there is some periodic merges to master) if needed.
title: eth/gasestimator: check ErrGasLimitTooHigh conditions

body:
Current `master` has a bit ambiguous API: 

```
func (c *ChainConfig) IsGrayGlacier(*big.Int) bool 
func (c *ChainConfig) IsCancun( *big.Int) bool 
func (c *ChainConfig) IsShanghai( *big.Int) bool 
...
```
Of these three, one of them is defined by time. Namely, `IsShanghai` takes a `*big.Int` representing `time`, the others take a `*big.Int` representing block `number`. Which clearly is an **error**, because `Cancun` comes after `Shanghai`, so _should_ be time-based. 

The way the API is defined, these types of errors can happen pretty easily. Someone can easily assume that `IsCancun` takes a timestamp (because it should), but it has not been wired that way internally. 

This PR changes the API so that `uint64` is used for `time`. The reason for this is that `types.Header` uses `uint64` for time.
title: all: make timestamp-based fork checks based on uint64

body: This PR changes Shanghai activation to be based on timestamps instead of block numbers

body:
This PR defines Cancun and Osaka forks as timestamp based forks which will happen
after Shanghai.

Apart from that, this PR changes three chain configs to `keyed fields` structure. Since
there are lots of forks/configs in a chain and will be more in the future, it's really unfriendly
for eyes to check which forks are activated and unintuitive to add more future forks.
title: params: define cancun and prague as timestamp based forks
